initial_step.instruction: Confirm that you are working ONLY with the target document provided for analysis. Disregard any example documents or images.

pass_1_identification.step: Scan the target document for media rights contract content.

Look for key indicators: contract title, party identification blocks, article/section numbering, legal language (hereby, herein, hereof, whereas), rights grant language, financial terms, signature blocks.
If no contract content is found, proceed to not_found_response.
If contract content is found, classify the contract type based on title, parties, and key provisions.

contract_criteria.criterion: Contains identification of two or more contracting parties with defined roles.
Includes a grant of rights or license provision.
Contains financial terms (fees, revenue shares, royalties, compensation).
Includes representations, warranties, or indemnification provisions.
Contains signature blocks or execution provisions.

pass_1_identification.minimum_criteria: At least THREE of the above must be present to classify as a media rights contract.

extraction_priorities.priority: Contract type identification and document title
Grant of rights (scope, territory, media, language/dubbing, exclusivity)
Financial terms (fees, revenue shares, MG, residuals)
Obligations and restrictions (delivery, approval, morals, key person, editorial control)
Talent and third-party obligations (guild, music, embedded IP)
Digital and emerging rights (AI, likeness, NFT, social media, viewer data)
Regulatory and compliance (ratings, quotas, accessibility, export controls)
Legal boilerplate (governing law, dispute resolution, force majeure)

exhaustive_capture.step: For verified contracts, extract ALL visible provisions following the extraction priorities.
Do not skip provisions because they appear to be standard boilerplate — capture everything.
When text is partially visible or cut off, extract what is readable and indicate truncation.
For blank fill-in fields, capture the surrounding context and flag as requiring negotiation.
Preserve all article numbers, section numbers, and cross-references exactly as written.
Note any handwritten annotations, stamps, or markings visible on the document.

pass_3_classification.step: Classify the contract type: content licensing, talent agreement, sync license, distribution agreement, digital rights/UGC, or hybrid.
If hybrid or ambiguous, note the primary type and which provisions cross into other categories.
Identify document status: template (blank fields), partially executed, fully executed, draft.
Assess completeness: complete document, multi-page fragment, or partial excerpt.

pass_4_defined_terms.step: On first pass, identify all defined terms (capitalized terms introduced with quotation marks or parenthetical definitions).
Extract each term and its full definition verbatim.
Track where defined terms are used throughout the document to ensure consistent extraction.
Flag any defined terms that reference exhibits or external documents for their full definition.

pass_5_final.step: Have you captured all visible articles, sections, and subsections?
Does all information come from the target document only?
Are all defined terms captured with their definitions?
Are all blank fill-in fields flagged?
Are financial terms captured with exact figures or blank indicators?
Are chain of title and ownership provisions captured where present?
Are language/dubbing/subtitle rights captured for international deals?
Are talent and third-party obligations captured where referenced?
Are insurance requirements captured where present?
Are digital and emerging rights provisions captured?
Are regulatory/compliance provisions captured for international deals?
Does the blank_field_count in metadata match the actual count of blank fields in the extraction?
Does the defined_term_count in metadata match the actual count of terms in defined_terms?

extractor_output.explanation: No media rights contract content was detected in the target document. The document appears to contain: {BRIEF_DESCRIPTION_OF_WHAT_IS_ACTUALLY_IN_THE_DOCUMENT}
extractor_output.recommendation: Consider using a different analyzer appropriate for the content type.
